cross_references.why_relevant_for_part4: Contains LSS1-5 cumulative capacity and auction results. Documents utility procurement mechanism as auction-based rather than RPO-based.
LSS auction price trend data. Benchmarks utility-scale solar at $33-61/MWh against CCGT and coal.
Sets top-level RE procurement targets (31% by 2025, 70% by 2050) that drive utility obligations. Legal basis for LSS programme continuation.
Covers TPA framework and ENEGEM cross-border trading platform — directly shapes how utilities procure and dispatch renewable energy.
Contains LSS1-4 auction results, capacity awarded by round, and tariff history from 2016 to 2023.
Quantifies Peninsular Malaysia's remaining grid absorption capacity (2.4GW before storage needed) and Sabah's near-exhausted quota (150MW).

content.title: Guidelines on Large Scale Solar Photovoltaic Plant for Connection to Electricity Networks

key_data.maaq: Maximum Annual Authorised Quantity — energy ceiling developer is paid for

content.summary: Official technical guidelines for connecting LSS plants to Malaysia's electricity network. Covers grid connection requirements, power system study (PSS) obligations, SPPA structure, licensing process, potential injection points, and critical milestones to commercial operation date (COD). Primary reference for any developer planning a utility-scale solar project.

rag.unknown_unknown_note: 51% Malaysian equity requirement applies to LSS PETRA Package 1 (1-10MW) — 100% Bumiputera required. Foreign investors must check package-specific ownership limits before applying. Not prominently stated in main guideline text.

content.title: TNB Sustainability Report 2024
content.summary: TNB's Sustainability Report 2024 covering grid investment plans, RE procurement obligations, and energy transition progress. Key data: RM45 billion grid investment 2025-2027, 2,500MW HHFS roadmap, CRESS framework launch September 2024, 300MW RE export cap to Singapore despite 1GW interconnection capacity.

rag.unknown_unknown_note: RE export to Singapore is capped at 300MW despite a 1GW interconnection line being available — 70% of capacity is underutilised. CRESS wheeling fee to TNB is not yet clearly defined, creating financial uncertainty for RE developers selling to corporate buyers.

content.title: Malaysia Energy Statistics Handbook 2023
content.summary: Official annual energy statistics handbook for Malaysia. Contains generation capacity by fuel type, electricity demand data, RE installed capacity breakdown, tariff structure, and grid infrastructure data for Peninsular Malaysia, Sabah, and Sarawak. Essential numerical baseline for understanding Malaysia's current utility landscape and solar integration headroom.

rag.unknown_unknown_note: Coal still dominates at 49% of generation despite 70% RE target by 2050. Solar contributes only 2.4% of actual dispatched electricity despite growing installed capacity — capacity factor gap (solar ~15% vs coal ~70%) is a key risk not highlighted in policy documents.
